introduction: Abnormalities of cardiac rhythm. Usually complicate acquired and congenital heart diseases

features: Features of complications/underlying causes (s): cardiac failure, stroke, structural heart diseases, etc

investigations: Atrial fibrillation is characterized by absence of p waves, presence of fibrillary waves and varying RR interval
Electrolytes including K and Mg; Urea and Creatinine

drug: Atrial fibrillation Rate Control: Digoxin (0.125 - 0.375 mg daily)
Prevention of atrial fibrillation: Amiodarone 100 - 400 mg daily
Anticoagulation (warfarin 5 mg daily; maintain target INR 2-2.5)
supportive_measures: Efficient systems to facilitate patient recovery

prevention: Prevention and prompt management of predisposing conditions such as hypertension, rheumatic heart disease, diabetes mellitus, ischaemic heart disease, congenital heart diseases etc
